Personal operating system · daily rhythm

Shape
the day.

Rhythm is not a productivity app. It is a calm, intelligent operating system for the shape of your day — blending presence, planning, sound, and motion into one continuous experience.

Explore the system Back to studio
A different kind of system

Not faster.
More present.

Most tools push you to do more. Rhythm asks something different — what does this day actually need? It shapes time around intention, not urgency. It brings focus without pressure, and rhythm without rigidity.

It is one system for the whole day. Not four apps in a folder.

Now

The present moment, held.

The Now screen knows the time, the weather, what matters most right now, and why — without asking you to think about it.

One screen. The whole shape of the moment.

Good afternoon, Sall. Training in 6 minutes. 13°, mainly clear. The Momentum Layer reads the day and surfaces the one thing that will make everything else feel lighter.

  • ✓
    Personalized greeting tied to time of day
  • ✓
    Momentum Layer — intelligent daily context, not just tasks
  • ✓
    Training day signal with countdown
  • ✓
    Weather woven into the emotional atmosphere
  • ✓
    Focus card: what matters, why it matters, one action

Timeline and sound, always in reach.

Scroll down and the day opens up — a live timeline of what is placed, a focus sound suggestion aligned to your current task, and space for intention. Everything visible, nothing competing.

  • ✓
    Live timeline with tasks placed in real time
  • ✓
    Sound state suggested based on current task
  • ✓
    Day intention — a quiet anchor beneath the noise
Flow

Shape the week gently.

Flow is where planning happens without pressure. Place what matters, let the day breathe, and keep the rest in rhythm.

A week view that feels calm, not crowded.

Tap a day. Place what matters. Add an intention beneath. Flow treats the week as something to be shaped — not a grid to be filled. The days breathe. The tasks sit lightly.

  • ✓
    Week arc — tap any day to open and shape it
  • ✓
    Month view toggle for wider perspective
  • ✓
    Tasks placed with time, not just listed
  • ✓
    Intention field — one line that anchors the day
  • ✓
    Calm, editorial layout — nothing feels urgent
Rhythm Sound

Sound states,
not playlists.

Rhythm treats sound as an environmental layer — something that holds the tone of the moment, not something you have to manage. Each state is a different way of being.

The sound follows the shape of your day.

Rhythm Sound reads your current phase — day in motion, focus block, recovery — and suggests a state. The orb shifts. The wave settles. The sound begins without a playlist.

  • ✓
    Phase-aware: the system knows if you are in work, rest, or transition
  • ✓
    Suggested state surfaces automatically — no searching
  • ✓
    Morning Brief and Night Review built into the rhythm
  • ✓
    Next task alert woven into the sound layer
State 01 ◌

Wake

A gentle opening layer. Still air and longer breath. Designed for the first 30 minutes when the day is still forming itself.

State 02 ◎

Focus

A cleaner field for work blocks. Rhythm suggests Focus when your task is active. Sound that supports attention instead of competing for it.

State 03 ◑

Meditation

A softer inward layer. Slower breathing, reset moments, and letting the day quiet down without disappearing. Morning I, Focus Reset, Still Return.

State 04 ●

Sleep

The day closes gently. A Night Review surfaces. Sound dims toward stillness. The system prepares for tomorrow's rhythm before you do.

Motion

Train with intent.
Recover with pride.

Motion is not a fitness tracker. It is the body layer of your daily rhythm — intelligent about timing, honest about recovery, and calm about progress.

The week feels shaped,
not chased.

Pull is ready. A session planned for 15:15. Motion tells you what the training focus is, when it happens, and how to read your week — without turning exercise into a dashboard problem.

  • ✓
    Today's Motion card — one clear training signal
  • ✓
    Push, Pull, Legs, Upper, Cardio, Full — chosen with intention
  • ✓
    Training Intelligence — reads rhythm, suggests bias, tracks recovery
  • ✓
    Week rhythm view — sessions shaped across the week, not just logged
  • ✓
    Weekly Proof — a quiet record of what was built
Why Rhythm

A new category.
Not a better app.

Rhythm is not competing with your calendar, your task manager, or your fitness tracker. It is operating at a different level entirely.

01

One system, not four

Now, Flow, Rhythm Sound, and Motion are not separate features. They are one continuous operating layer that reads the shape of your day and moves with it.

02

Emotional intelligence

Rhythm understands that a day has tone, not just tasks. The Momentum Layer, sound states, and morning brief are all built around how the day actually feels, not just what needs doing.

03

Intentional timing

Everything in Rhythm is time-aware. Sound shifts with the phase of the day. Motion knows when to push and when to recover. Flow places tasks in time, not just in a list.

04

Premium restraint

No notifications asking for attention. No streaks used as pressure. No gamification. Rhythm works quietly — and only surfaces what actually matters at this moment.

05

A companion, not a tracker

Rhythm does not measure you. It supports you. The Weekly Proof, the Night Review, the Momentum Layer — these are for clarity and calm, not performance anxiety.

06

Built to feel different

The arc, the orb, the wave, the gold accent — every visual decision is intentional. Rhythm should feel like entering a state, not opening a dashboard.

Begin

Your day
has a shape.

Most tools ignore it. Rhythm is built around it. For the people who want to move through the day with more clarity, more calm, and more of themselves intact by the end of it.
